phases.name: Foundation + Agent/Hub Core
phases.description: Monorepo scaffold, shared types/protocol, agent PTY daemon, Hub server with SQLite, WebSocket relay, session CRUD, Docker deployment
phases.goal: Sessions can be started via CLI, tracked by Hub, output streamed and recorded. docker compose up runs everything.

phases.name: Dashboard + Notifications + Approvals
phases.description: Nuxt 3 + Vuetify dashboard with live terminal, session/machine views, PTY input, approval system, notification engine
phases.goal: Full live monitoring from browser, approve/deny agent actions remotely, get notified on completion.

phases.name: Replay + Queue + Resume
phases.description: Recording playback, queue management with auto-advance, session resume/follow-up, session templates
phases.goal: Complete async workflow management — queue tasks, replay sessions, resume conversations.

phases.name: Polish + Power Features
phases.description: Multi-session grid, cross-machine queue, search/filter, secret scrubbing, recording retention, machine health charts
phases.goal: Multi-session power user workflow with operational polish.

phases.name: Workforce Foundation
phases.description: Repository registry, workspace manager, Job model, environment resolver, git worktree management
phases.goal: Spawn agents against any codebase — clone, branch, install deps, run agent, commit, PR.

phases.name: GitHub Integration
phases.description: GitHub App setup wizard, PR lifecycle, issue linking, webhook receiver, Checks API
phases.goal: Full GitHub integration — auto-create PRs, track reviews, report agent status as checks.

phases.name: Workforce Intelligence
phases.description: Auto-scheduling, batch jobs, cost tracking/budgets, scheduled tasks (cron), job dependencies
phases.goal: Intelligent workload management — auto-place jobs, enforce budgets, run recurring tasks.

phases.name: Advanced Isolation + Scaling
phases.description: Docker execution backend via spawnClaudeCodeProcess, devcontainer support, cloud sandbox integration
phases.goal: Run Claude Code in Docker containers for isolation and reproducibility.

epics.title: Monorepo & Build Infrastructure
epics.description: pnpm workspace with 4 packages (shared, agent, hub, dashboard), Turborepo pipeline, shared tsconfig, vitest setup, ESLint, Dockerfile scaffolds, docker-compose.yml
acceptanceCriteria: pnpm workspace resolves all 4 packages
turbo build succeeds for all packages
turbo prune --docker generates correct output for hub and agent
vitest runs with shared config
TypeScript strict mode enabled across all packages
docker compose build succeeds
docker compose up starts Hub serving on port 7700

epics.title: Shared Protocol & Types
epics.description: Zod schemas for all WebSocket message types (agent→hub, hub→agent, hub→dashboard), TypeScript type inference via z.infer, session/machine/queue record types, config schemas
acceptanceCriteria: All WebSocket message types defined with Zod schemas
Agent→Hub messages: register, heartbeat, session:started/output/ended, recording:upload, queue:updated
Hub→Agent messages: session:start/resume/kill/input, queue:add/remove/reorder
Hub→Dashboard messages: session:output/updated, machine:updated, queue:updated, notification
SessionRecord, MachineRecord, QueueTask, NotificationRecord types exported
Config schemas for agent and hub configuration
All types importable as @chq/shared

epics.title: Agent PTY Manager
epics.description: node-pty wrapper managing multiple concurrent PTY sessions, session lifecycle (spawn, stream output, kill), output buffering and chunking for WebSocket transmission
acceptanceCriteria: Spawn Claude Code PTY with configurable command, cwd, flags
Stream PTY output via callback with timestamp+data chunks
Write input to PTY stdin
Kill PTY with SIGTERM then SIGKILL after timeout
Track session state: spawning, running, completed, failed
Capture exit code and Claude session ID
Configurable max concurrent sessions per agent
Use -n flag for session naming: chq:<machineId>:<sessionId>

epics.title: Agent Daemon & CLI
epics.description: Long-running daemon process with WebSocket client to Hub, heartbeat reporting, config loading, CLI commands (chq run, chq agent start/stop/status, chq sessions, chq kill)
acceptanceCriteria: chq agent start launches daemon, connects to Hub via WebSocket
chq agent stop gracefully shuts down (waits for active sessions)
chq agent status shows running sessions + connection status
chq run 'prompt' spawns a session via the daemon
chq run --cwd /path spawns in specific directory
chq sessions lists active sessions
chq kill <id> kills a specific session
Heartbeat sends CPU/mem/disk every 30s
Auto-reconnect to Hub with exponential backoff
Agent writes HTTP hooks config to ~/.claude/settings.json on startup
Config loaded from ~/.chq/config.json with Zod validation

epics.title: Agent Recording & Upload
epics.description: Terminal output recording to JSONL format, buffered upload to Hub, chunked streaming with configurable intervals
acceptanceCriteria: Record PTY output as JSONL with {ts, data} entries
Buffer chunks for configurable interval (default 100ms)
Upload chunks to Hub via WebSocket (agent:recording:upload)
Send final=true on session end to close recording
Handle upload failures gracefully (retry, local buffer)

epics.title: Hub Server Foundation
epics.description: Fastify server with plugin architecture, @fastify/websocket for WS support, @fastify/static for serving dashboard, pino logging, config management, health endpoint
acceptanceCriteria: Fastify server starts on configurable port (default 7700)
WebSocket paths: /ws/agent and /ws/dashboard
@fastify/static serves dashboard from configured directory
SPA fallback: GET /* returns index.html for client-side routing
GET /health returns {status: 'ok', version, uptime}
pino structured logging (never console.log)
Config from env vars + config file with Zod validation
Graceful shutdown on SIGTERM

epics.title: Hub SQLite Database
epics.description: better-sqlite3 with WAL mode, schema migrations, machines/sessions/queue/notifications tables, prepared statement DAL
acceptanceCriteria: Database created at configurable path with WAL mode
PRAGMA settings: synchronous=NORMAL, cache_size=-20000, mmap_size=256MB, busy_timeout=5000, foreign_keys=ON
Migration system: numbered SQL files applied on startup
Tables: machines, sessions, queue, notifications, notification_config
All queries use parameterized prepared statements (never string interpolation)
Transactions for multi-statement operations
INTEGER for timestamps (Unix epoch), TEXT for UUIDs

epics.title: Hub WebSocket Relay & Agent Handler
epics.description: Agent WebSocket connection management, message routing, session output relay to recordings + dashboard subscribers, agent registration and health tracking
acceptanceCriteria: Accept agent WebSocket connections at /ws/agent
Parse all incoming messages with Zod schemas from @chq/shared
Handle agent:register — create/update machine record
Handle agent:heartbeat — update machine last_seen, CPU/mem
Handle agent:session:started — create session record
Handle agent:session:output — append to recording file, forward to dashboard subscribers
Handle agent:session:ended — update session record with exit code
Handle agent:recording:upload — append chunks to JSONL file
Track connected agents by machineId
Detect agent disconnect, mark machine offline after 60s

epics.title: Hub REST API — Sessions & Machines
epics.description: REST endpoints for session CRUD, machine listing, session start/kill/input commands relayed to agents
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/machines — list registered agents + status
GET /api/machines/:id — machine detail + active sessions
GET /api/sessions — list all sessions (filterable: ?machine=X&status=running)
GET /api/sessions/:id — session detail
POST /api/sessions — start new session (validates agent capacity, sends hub:session:start)
DELETE /api/sessions/:id — kill running session (sends hub:session:kill)
POST /api/sessions/:id/input — send PTY input (sends hub:session:input)
Request body validation with Zod
Proper HTTP status codes: 200, 201, 400, 404, 500

epics.title: Hub Hook Receiver & Recording Storage
epics.description: HTTP endpoints receiving Claude Code hooks (Stop, PreToolUse, PostToolUse, SubagentStop), session_events table, recording file management on disk
acceptanceCriteria: POST /hooks/stop — update session status, log event
POST /hooks/pre-tool-use — log event, forward to dashboard
POST /hooks/post-tool-use — log event, forward to dashboard
POST /hooks/subagent-stop — log event
session_events table stores hook payloads with session_id + timestamp
Recording files stored at {dataDir}/recordings/{sessionId}.jsonl
GET /api/sessions/:id/recording — stream JSONL file
GET /api/sessions/:id/recording/meta — recording metadata (duration, size)

epics.title: Dashboard Foundation
epics.description: Nuxt 3 SPA scaffold with Vuetify 3, default layout (VAppBar + VNavigationDrawer + VMain), dark/light theme, static build integrated into Hub Docker image
acceptanceCriteria: Nuxt 3 with ssr:false, Vuetify 3 module configured
Default layout: VAppBar (logo, title), VNavigationDrawer (nav items), VMain
Navigation: Overview, Sessions, Machines, Queue links
Dark/light theme toggle in app bar
nuxt generate produces static SPA in .output/public/
Static files served by Hub via @fastify/static in production
Relative API paths (/api/*, /ws/*) work without configuration

epics.title: Dashboard WebSocket Client
epics.description: Reconnecting WebSocket composable with Zod message parsing, session subscription/unsubscription, connection state management
acceptanceCriteria: useWebSocket composable connects to /ws/dashboard
Auto-reconnect with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, max 30s)
Connection state ref: connecting, connected, disconnected, error
Parse incoming messages with Zod schemas from @chq/shared
subscribe/unsubscribe methods for session and machine resources
Type-safe send method
Clean disconnection in composable cleanup

epics.title: Dashboard Terminal View
epics.description: xterm.js terminal component for live session output rendering, @xterm/addon-fit for responsive resize, @xterm/addon-webgl with DOM fallback, PTY input bar
acceptanceCriteria: TerminalView.vue wraps @xterm/xterm with @xterm/addon-fit and @xterm/addon-webgl
WebGL addon loaded in try/catch with DOM renderer fallback
ResizeObserver triggers fitAddon.fit() on container resize
Terminal receives session output via WebSocket subscription
terminal.write(chunk.data) for each output chunk
Terminal disposed in onUnmounted (no memory leaks)
TerminalInput.vue: text field + Enter to send PTY input via POST /api/sessions/:id/input
Session state indicator: Running, Waiting for Input, Completed, Failed

epics.title: Dashboard Session & Machine Pages
epics.description: Overview page with machine cards and session list, machine detail page, session detail page with terminal, Pinia stores for sessions and machines
acceptanceCriteria: Overview page (/) shows machine cards with status, session count, CPU/mem
Machine card click navigates to /machines/:id
Machine detail shows active sessions, completed sessions, machine info
Session list with status badges, quick action icons (terminal, kill)
Session detail (/sessions/:id) shows TerminalView + metadata panel
Start session button: modal with machine, prompt, cwd inputs
Kill session button with confirmation
Pinia stores: sessions (setup syntax), machines (setup syntax)
Three states everywhere: loading (VSkeletonLoader), empty (VAlert), error (VAlert + retry)

epics.title: Hub Dashboard WebSocket Handler
epics.description: Dashboard WebSocket connection management, session output forwarding, subscribe/unsubscribe semantics, session/machine state broadcast
acceptanceCriteria: Accept dashboard connections at /ws/dashboard
Handle subscribe/unsubscribe messages for sessions and machines
Forward session:output only to subscribed dashboard clients
Broadcast session:updated when session state changes
Broadcast machine:updated when machine state changes
Send initial state on subscription (current session/machine data)

epics.title: Approval System
epics.description: approval_requests + approval_policy_rules tables, policy evaluation engine, PreToolUse/PermissionRequest hook handling, approval REST API, approval queue page, inline approval banner, AskUser reply UI
acceptanceCriteria: approval_requests table with full schema from research
approval_policy_rules table with priority-ordered rules
Default policy rules shipped: auto-approve reads, auto-deny dangerous, require approval for rest
Policy evaluation: deny→ask→allow, first match wins
POST /hooks/permission-request receives hook, evaluates policy, returns decision
Agent canUseTool callback routes to Hub for SDK sessions
GET/POST /api/approvals — list pending, respond with decision
POST /api/approvals/bulk/respond — bulk approve/deny
Dashboard /approvals page with queue, risk badges, countdown timers
Inline approval banner in session view
AskUser reply with text input (chat-like UI)
Approve & Remember creates policy rule from decision
Timeout sweeper auto-denies expired approvals (default 5min)
Notification on pending approval (toast + badge count)

epics.title: Notification Engine
epics.description: Webhook dispatch for session events, dashboard push via WebSocket, notification config CRUD, notification history, toast display
acceptanceCriteria: notification_config table with webhook URLs, event filters, format (json/discord/slack)
Dispatch webhooks on: session_completed, session_failed, session_stalled, queue_empty, agent_offline, input_needed
Dashboard receives notification via WebSocket, shows VSnackbar toast
Notification feed sidebar/panel with history
GET/PUT /api/notifications/config
GET /api/notifications/history

epics.title: Session Replay Player
epics.description: xterm.js recording playback with timeline scrubber, play/pause, speed controls (1x/2x/4x/8x), jump to timestamp, @xterm/addon-serialize for scrub
acceptanceCriteria: Load JSONL recording via GET /api/sessions/:id/recording
Build timeline index from timestamps
Play/pause with spacebar shortcut
Speed controls: 1x, 2x, 4x, 8x
Timeline scrubber with activity heatmap
Jump to timestamp (click on timeline)
Session events from hooks overlaid on timeline (tool use markers, sub-agent activity)

epics.title: Queue Management
epics.description: Agent-side queue with auto-advance, Hub queue CRUD API, dashboard queue page with drag-to-reorder, priority management
acceptanceCriteria: Agent persists queue to disk, auto-advances when PTY slot frees
chq run --queue adds to queue instead of immediate start
GET /api/queues, GET /api/queues/:machineId
POST /api/queues/:machineId — add task
DELETE /api/queues/:machineId/:taskId — remove task
PATCH /api/queues/:machineId — reorder queue
Dashboard queue page with drag-to-reorder (vuedraggable)
Queue depth shown on machine cards

epics.title: Session Resume & Follow-up
epics.description: Claude session ID extraction, --resume flow for completed sessions, session chaining (parent→child), dashboard follow-up prompt UI
acceptanceCriteria: Agent extracts Claude session ID from stream-json output or ~/.claude/projects/
POST /api/sessions/:id/resume — creates new session with --resume flag
New session linked via parent_session_id
Dashboard shows follow-up prompt input on completed sessions
Session chain view: original → follow-up → follow-up

epics.title: Session Templates
epics.description: templates table, template CRUD API, template gallery in dashboard, launch session from template with variable substitution
acceptanceCriteria: templates table: name, description, prompt, cwd, flags, variables
CRUD API: GET/POST/PUT/DELETE /api/templates
POST /api/templates/:id/launch — create session from template with variable values
Dashboard template gallery (card layout)
Variable substitution: {{variable}} in prompt replaced at launch
Variables render as form fields in launch modal

epics.title: Enhanced Session & Machine Schema
epics.description: Add tags, timeout_seconds, max_cost_usd, capabilities, requirements, cost tracking columns to existing tables
acceptanceCriteria: sessions: add tags, timeout_seconds, max_cost_usd, cost_usd, tokens_used, created_by
machines: add capabilities (JSON array)
queue: add tags, timeout_seconds, max_cost_usd, requirements, depends_on, name, retry_policy
Session status enum expanded: queued, blocked, running, completed, failed, cancelled
API supports filtering by tags: ?tag=project:my-app

epics.title: Multi-Session Grid View
epics.description: 2x2 or 1x4 layout of live terminal views, click to expand, compact metadata overlays, synchronized resize
acceptanceCriteria: /sessions/grid page with configurable layout (2x2, 1x4)
Each cell is a TerminalView with compact session info overlay
Click cell to expand to full session view
Synchronized resize handling across all terminals
Session selector to choose which sessions to display

epics.title: Cross-Machine Queue & Session Search
epics.description: Move tasks between machine queues, session search/filter/history view, bulk operations
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/queues/:machineId/:taskId/move — move task to different machine
Dashboard cross-machine queue view with drag between columns
Session search: text search across prompts, filter by status/machine/tags
Session history view with pagination
Bulk actions: select multiple sessions, bulk kill/clone

epics.title: Secret Scrubbing & Recording Retention
epics.description: Regex-based secret scrubbing in recordings before storage/streaming, configurable recording retention with cleanup cron
acceptanceCriteria: Agent config: scrubPatterns regex array, scrubReplacement string
PTY output scrubbed before streaming to Hub and before recording
Hub retention policy: configurable max age (days) and max size (GB)
Cleanup runs on schedule (configurable interval)
Dashboard notification config page

epics.title: Machine Health Charts
epics.description: CPU/memory/disk time-series data collection, sparkline charts on machine cards, detailed charts on machine detail page
acceptanceCriteria: Hub stores health history from heartbeats (time-bucketed)
Machine card sparklines for CPU and memory (last 30 min)
Machine detail page: CPU, memory, disk charts (last 24h)
Chart library: lightweight (unovis or Chart.js)

epics.title: Rich Notification Integrations
epics.description: Discord embeds, Slack Block Kit, ntfy.sh push, browser Notification API with service worker, notification sound option
acceptanceCriteria: Discord webhook format with rich embeds (color by severity, fields)
Slack webhook format with Block Kit (sections, actions)
ntfy.sh integration with priority levels and click URL
Browser Notification API via service worker (2 action buttons)
Notification sound toggle in settings
Notification grouping: batch approvals within 5s window

epics.title: Repository Registry
epics.description: repos table, CRUD API, auto-detection of package manager/node version/setup commands, repo import from GitHub URL
acceptanceCriteria: repos table: url, name, auth_method, preferred_machine_id, dependency_manager, setup_commands, pre_flight_commands, tags
CRUD API: GET/POST/PUT/DELETE /api/repos
POST /api/repos/import — auto-fill from GitHub URL
POST /api/repos/:id/detect — auto-detect package manager, node version, setup commands
Dashboard Repos page with card list

epics.title: Workspace Manager
epics.description: workspaces table, git clone/worktree management on agents, workspace lifecycle (create→prepare→ready→active→cleanup), dependency installation
acceptanceCriteria: workspaces table tracking all workspaces across machines
Agent workspace provisioner: clone (partial clone), create worktree, install deps
Git worktree for parallel agent work on same repo
Environment resolver: detect package manager (nypm), node version (fnm)
Hub→Agent: hub:workspace:provision, hub:workspace:cleanup
Agent→Hub: agent:workspace:ready, agent:workspace:error
Cleanup policies: configurable TTL, max per machine, max disk

epics.title: Job Model & Orchestrator
epics.description: jobs table, Job state machine (pending→provisioning→preparing→running→post_processing→completed), Job API, full workflow from repo to PR
acceptanceCriteria: jobs table with full schema from research
Job state machine: pending→provisioning→preparing→running→post_processing→completed/failed
POST /api/jobs — create job (triggers full workflow)
GET /api/jobs/:id — job detail + sessions + workspace + PR
POST /api/jobs/:id/retry, /cancel, /follow-up
Hub orchestrates: select machine → provision workspace → spawn session → post-completion
Job links to sessions via session.job_id
Dashboard Job launcher modal

epics.title: Dashboard Workforce Views
epics.description: Repos page, Job launcher, Workspaces page, enhanced Overview with active jobs and cost
acceptanceCriteria: Repos page: card list with sync status, active jobs, quick actions
Job launcher modal: repo + prompt + branch + machine + advanced options
Workspaces page: table of active workspaces, disk usage, cleanup actions
Enhanced overview: active jobs (not just sessions), cost today, PRs open
Sidebar navigation updated with Repos, Jobs, Workspaces

epics.title: Agent Git Operations
epics.description: simple-git integration for clone, branch, commit, push, status, log, diff. Branch naming convention chq/<id>/<slug>. Pre/post-flight checks.
acceptanceCriteria: simple-git wrapper for clone (partial clone), fetch, branch, checkout, commit, push
Git worktree add/remove/prune
Branch naming: chq/<session-id>/<description-slug>
Pre-flight: run configured commands, report pass/fail
Post-flight: run configured commands, commit remaining changes
Git status reporting to Hub via WebSocket

epics.title: GitHub App Integration
epics.description: GitHub App manifest flow setup wizard, @octokit/auth-app for authentication, Tailscale Funnel for webhooks, PAT fallback
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard GitHub setup wizard (7-step flow)
Manifest flow: POST to GitHub → callback with credentials → store encrypted
Installation management: store installation_id per org
@octokit/auth-app for JWT creation + token auto-rotation
PAT fallback with clear limitation warnings
GitHub App private key stored as Docker secret
Test endpoint to verify GitHub connection

epics.title: GitHub PR Lifecycle
epics.description: Auto-create PRs from completed jobs, PR tracking table, Checks API reporting, webhook receiver for PR events
acceptanceCriteria: Auto-create PR on job completion (configurable per job)
PR body: prompt, diff summary, test results, cost, recording link
pull_requests table tracking PR status, review status, CI status
Checks API: create check run on commit, update on completion
POST /api/github/webhooks — receive PR/push/check events
@octokit/webhooks for signature verification
Dashboard PR tracker page
POST /api/jobs/:id/create-pr — manual PR creation

epics.title: GitHub Issue Linking
epics.description: Link jobs to GitHub issues, auto-comment on issue progress, close issue on PR merge
acceptanceCriteria: Job creation accepts github_issue_number
Auto-comment on issue: 'Claude HQ is working on this'
Auto-comment on completion with summary + PR link
Optionally close issue on PR merge (configurable)

epics.title: Auto-Scheduling & Machine Selection
epics.description: Scoring algorithm for machine selection when machineId is omitted, capability/requirement matching, load balancing
acceptanceCriteria: machineId optional on session/job creation
Score: (free slots * 10) + (100 - cpu%) + (100 - mem%) - (queue_depth * 5)
Filter by requirements matching machine capabilities
Fallback to any available machine if preferred unavailable

epics.title: Batch Jobs & Cost Dashboard
epics.description: Batch job launcher (same prompt across multiple repos), cost tracking per session/job/repo/machine, budget thresholds with alerts
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/jobs/batch — create jobs for multiple repos with shared prompt
Batch progress tracking in dashboard
session_costs table with token breakdown per model
Cost aggregation: by repo, by machine, by day/week/month
Budget config: per-session, per-machine daily, global daily
Budget alerts at 50/75/90/100% thresholds
Dashboard cost page with charts

epics.title: Scheduled Tasks (Cron)
epics.description: scheduled_tasks table, cron expression support, concurrency policy, history limits, dashboard management UI
acceptanceCriteria: scheduled_tasks table: cron_expression, prompt, cwd, machine_id, concurrency_policy
Hub cron scheduler checks every minute and enqueues matching tasks
Concurrency policies: allow, forbid, replace
History limits: configurable successful/failed job retention
Dashboard scheduled tasks page with enable/disable toggle

epics.title: Job Dependencies & Retry Policies
epics.description: dependsOn field for task chains, retry with exponential backoff, job groups (sequential/parallel)
acceptanceCriteria: Queue tasks support dependsOn: array of task IDs
Task eligible when all dependencies completed with exit 0
Failed dependencies mark dependent tasks as 'blocked'
Retry policy: maxRetries, backoffSeconds, retryOnExitCodes
Hub auto-requeues failed tasks per retry policy
Job groups table for sequential/parallel cross-repo workflows

epics.title: Docker Execution Backend
epics.description: spawnClaudeCodeProcess routing to Docker containers, pre-running container pool, API key injection, volume mounts for workspaces
acceptanceCriteria: Agent config: executionMode (native | docker)
Docker spawn: child_process.spawn('docker', ['exec', '-i', ...]) satisfies SpawnedProcess
No -t flag (TTY corrupts JSON-lines protocol)
Pre-running container management (warm pool)
API key injected via -e flag per exec
Workspace path mapped to container volume mount
Signal forwarding (SIGTERM → container process)

epics.title: Devcontainer & Cloud Sandbox Support
epics.description: Run agents inside project devcontainers, cloud sandbox integration (E2B, Daytona, Cloudflare), SSH spawn pattern
acceptanceCriteria: Detect .devcontainer/devcontainer.json in repo
Build/pull devcontainer image for repo
Route Claude Code execution into devcontainer
SSH spawn: child_process.spawn('ssh', ['-o', 'BatchMode=yes', ...]) pattern
Cloud sandbox integration points documented

protocol.description: WebSocket message types and shared types defined

pty-spawn.description: Agent can spawn and manage PTY sessions

agent-daemon.description: Agent runs as daemon with CLI interface

overview-page.description: Dashboard overview with machines and sessions

docker-execution.description: Run Claude Code in Docker containers
